name: Guided cognitive trap lab
layoutPrinciple: Pair the source document with a guided analysis rail so learners move from context to assumptions, evidence, traps, mitigations, and expert synthesis in a fixed instructional sequence.
interactionPatterns: split layout with source panel and analysis panel
bias badges linked to deeper explanation cards
expert walkthrough revealed after independent analysis
uiNotes: Keep the source text visually heavy enough that learners still read it, not just the commentary.
Use a numbered phase rail to reinforce analysis order.
Treat evidence, biases, and mitigations as separate teaching moves instead of one blended block.
Expert analysis should feel like a reveal or walkthrough, not the first thing the learner sees.
Preserve the teaching-artifact feel; do not turn this pattern into a generic dashboard.
deidentificationNotes: Derived from a classroom HTML artifact after removing instructor identity, course identifiers, and source-specific branding.
Preserve reusable interface structure and instructional flow, not personal or institution-specific metadata.
